content.problemStatement:
Congrats if you have made this far. This is where all the previous features come together and we add the final layer of moderator powers and system constraints. 
We will now grant moderators special permissions that override normal user limitations. Implement the final system constraints for maximum reply depth and total replies per thread. \
**After this final task, the application will be feature-complete.** \
**Moderator Powers:**
- A user with "(Moderator)" status will see "Edit", "Delete", and "Hide" buttons on all comments, not just their own.
- They can edit any comment at any time, bypassing the 5-minute limit.
- When they delete someone else's comment, it will display a special message: "[This comment was deleted by a moderator]".
- They can hide any comment, which will display a "[This comment was hidden by a moderator]" message. - - They can also unhide comments. \
**System Constraints:**
- The "Reply" button will no longer appear on comments that are already 5 levels deep.
- The system will block any attempt to post a reply to a thread that already contains 50 or more total comments (top-level comment + all its descendants), showing an error message.
### Files to Create/Modify for this Task:
- comment_system/settings.py **(Done for you)**
   - Add the final two settings to control the reply limits. *Check the end of the file*
- comments/models.py 
   - Update the Comment model significantly. 
   - Add new status flags and update the permission methods to give moderators power. 
   - Add new helper methods to enforce constraints.
- comments/views.py 
   - Update existing views to handle moderator logic and add new views for moderator-specific actions. 
   - The add_comment view gets the new constraint logic.
- comments/urls.py 
   - Add the URLs for the new moderator actions.
- comments/templates/comments/comment_display.html **(Done for you)**
   - Pass the MAX_REPLY_LEVEL setting into the template context so it can be used by comment_display.html.
- comments/templates/comments/page_detail.html **(Done for you)**
   - This template gets the final, most complex logic.

**After completing the above**
- Click on **RUN** and open the browser on a new tab. 

**Test the following features**: 
- **Become a Moderator:** As a normal user, post 10 comments. As chef, upvote all the 10 comments. Log back in as the normal user; they should now be a moderator.
- **Test Mod Powers:** As the new moderator, find a comment by another user (chef). Test that you can edit it, delete it (check for the special message), and hide/unhide it.
- **Test Reply Depth:** Create a chain of replies, one after another. The "Reply" button should disappear on the 5th-level reply.
- **Test Thread Limit:** Find a top-level comment and add replies until the total thread count is 50. The next attempt to reply anywhere in that thread should be blocked with an error message.

### CONGRATS!! You have successfully completed the Comment System App using Django.
